rationale: The one substantive ground-truth signal (yJader's inline comment) explains that dropping the already_submitted gate is correct and intentional, since both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts identically. Y independently verifies this exact fact by reading stage_pool.py and reaches the same conclusion, plus correctly diagnoses the outputs[0]→outputs fix as resolving a real TypeError regression — matching the real reviewers' unanimous APPROVE/LGTM verdict. X never checks stage_pool.py and instead flags the already_submitted removal and the outputs return-value change as 'major' unverified risks, directly contradicting the ground-truth reasoning, and lands on REQUEST CHANGES against a PR that was cleanly approved — its findings are speculative rather than grounded despite being individually actionable.
